name: My Favorite Curried Shrimp Dish 10/10/96
recipeIngredient: 4 Small hot red chiles (such as hontaka), dried
2 large Poblano or Anaheim chiles, stemmed and seeded
1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Drops of lemon juice to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy skillet, dry roast the chiles, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cloves, and cardamom until fragrant.
2. Place the roasted spices in a coffee or spice grinder and grind to a fine powder.
3. Mix the ground spices with enough water to form a paste. Set aside.
4. Heat olive oil in a pan and saute the onions, ginger, garlic, and turmeric until crisp-tender.
5. Add coconut milk and Poblano chiles to the pan and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer for 3-4 minutes.
7. Add tomatoes and shrimp, then simmer until shrimp are just cooked (approximately 3 minutes).
8. Season with salt and lemon juice as desired.
9. Serve immediately garnished with cilantro leaves and steamed rice.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings
